data.title: ETFs with heavy weightings in Taiwan Semi catch a ride higher
data.description:
Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (NYSE:TSM) have soared on Thursday morning after the world's largest contract chipmaker delivered strong earnings, which in turn pushed many exchange-traded funds higher as TSM is popular among ETFs.

Of the 83 ETFs that hold TSM, nine of the names have portfolio allocations towards TSM of 10% or greater. All nine funds find themselves higher on the day thanks in part to TSM. Outlined below are the nine funds along with each of their allocations towards TSM. The list is ranked by weighting percentages.

No. 9: Global X Emerging Markets Great Consumer ETF (EMC), 10.00% weighting.

No. 8: Wahed Dow Jones Islamic World ETF (UMMA), 10.79% weighting.

No. 7: Freedom 100 Emerging Markets ETF (FRDM), 11.43% weighting.

No. 6: Matthews Pacific Tiger Active ETF (ASIA), 11.60% weighting.

No. 5: National Security Emerging Markets Index ETF (NSI), 12.50% weighting.

No. 4: VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH), 12.88% weighting.

No. 3: Matthews Emerging Markets Ex China Active ETF (MEMX), 14.37% weighting.

No. 2: SP Funds S&P Global Technology ETF (SPTE), 15.54% weighting.

No. 1: SP Funds S&P World ETF (SPWO), 16.43% weighting.

For reference, shares of TSM are higher by 11.1% after the global foundry reported third-quarter results and guidance that surpassed expectations, amid increased demand for artificial intelligence chips.

"Our business in the third quarter was supported by strong smartphone and AI-related demand for our industry-leading 3nm and 5nm technologies," said TSM's Senior VP and CFO Wendell Huang.

data.title: Alcoa turns lower despite swinging to Q3 profit on higher alumina prices
data.description:
Alcoa (NYSE:AA) -1.2% in early trading Thursday following a surge of as much as 10% pre-market after easily beating expectations for Q3 adjusted earnings, which analysts said were aided by surging alumina prices, and reaching a proposed agreement to secure the viability of its San Ciprian, Spain, alumina and aluminum plant.

Q3 net income swung to $90M, or $0.38/share, from a loss of $168M, or a $0.94/share loss, in the year-earlier quarter, while revenues rose 11.6% Y/Y to $2.9B.

Q3 alumina production fell 4% Q/Q to 2.44M metric tons primarily due to the full curtailment of the Kwinana refinery completed in June 2024, while aluminum segment production rose 3% to 559K metric tons primarily due to continued progress on the Alumar smelter restart.

Q3 third-party alumina shipments fell 9% Q/Q primarily due to decreased trading, and total aluminum shipments declined 6% primarily due to decreased trading and the timing of shipments.

Alcoa (AA) anticipates improved performance in its alumina segment in Q4, CFO Molly Beerman said on its earnings conference call, forecasting FY 2024 alumina segment shipments will reach 12.9M-13.1M metric tons, up 200K tons from previous projections due to increased trading volumes.

Alcoa's (AA) Q3 results were solid, as alumina tightness is a positive for the near-term outlook, and the San Ciprian partnership could lead to a favorable resolution, J.P. Morgan's Bill Peterson said, raising his stock price target to $39 from $36 while maintaining a Neutral rating.

data.title: Jefferies’ updated ‘Franchise picks’: CPAY, KVUE, SAIA, PFGC
data.description:
Jefferies analysts updated their “Franchise Picks” with four new additions and four subtractions.

These are 21 of Jefferies’ highest-conviction, buy-rated U.S. stocks that have valuation levels that suggest upside.

Analysts added Corpay (CPAY), Kenvue (KVUE), Performance Food Group (PFGC), and Saia (SAIA), and removed PepsiCo (PEP), STAG Industrial (STAG), Sysco Corp. (SYY), and Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies Corp. (WAB).

Since its inception, the Jefferies Franchise Pick list has reached almost 280% total return, compared to about 270% for the S&P 500 (SP500).

Here is the list, with their 2025 EPS estimates by Jefferies analysts:

data.title: Macy's launches holiday collection featuring Mickey and Minnie Mouse
data.description:
Macy’s (NYSE:M) is collaborating with Disney (NYSE:DIS) this holiday season with limited edition Mickey and Minnie Mouse merchandise, along with the debut of a Minnie Mouse character balloon at the Macy’s (M) Thanksgiving Day Parade.

The Mickey and Minnie collection for kids and adults will feature Disney (DIS) artwork by Jeff Shelly, Disney’s legendary director of character art.

“Macy’s and Disney have created magic together for ninety years, establishing a legacy that is beloved across multiple generations,” said Nata Dvir, chief merchandising officer at Macy’s. “We recognize this rich history and are excited to offer our customers this exclusive holiday assortment.”

Beginning October 17 through the New Year, Macy’s Herald Square flagship store and 200 additional stores across the country will have a dedicated Mickey and Minnie Shop with Herald Square hosting a special concession stand and interactive AR experience. LEGO will also host customer build events in select Macy’s (M) stores on Saturday, November 2.

The publicity and festivities surrounding the Mickey and Minnie collection hope to breathe life into the struggling department store which recently said it would hire fewer employees this holiday season, attributed to the softer consumer environment, inflation, and what is expected to be the slowest holiday sales season in six years.

Shares are up 1.5% on Thursday for a third consecutive day in the green.

data.title: Mawson Infrastructure reports September revenue of $4.15M
data.description:
• Mawson Infrastructure Group (NASDAQ:MIGI) on Thursday said its total monthly revenue increased 65% Y/Y to $4.15M in September 2024.
  • The company added that its digital colocation monthly business revenue was $3.36M in September 2024, up from zero in Sep 2023.
  • Mawson's energy management monthly business revenue was $0.62M in September, a 271% Y/Y increase. The company reported Self-mining bitcoin monthly business revenue of $0.18M.
  • MIGI is -6.16% to $1.37.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Optimism among retail investors falls in latest AAII survey
data.description:
Investors’ optimism about the short-term outlook for the stock market decreased this week, showed the latest AAII Sentiment survey.

A total of 45.5% of respondents, as per the AAII Sentiment survey, showed optimism, compared to 49% a week earlier. The one-year bullish high stands at 52.9%, while the historical average is 37.5%.

The percentage of those surveyed who felt bearish also went up for the week ended Oct 16, with the figure standing at 25.4% versus 20.6% last week. Neutral sentiment stood at 29.2% for the week, versus 30.4% last week, who saw no change in market direction.

Investors are looking at another earnings season, with many high-profile companies, including UnitedHealth (UNH) and ASML (ASML) showing disappointing results. UnitedHealth set the upper end of its 2025 earnings outlook below Wall Street’s expectations, while ASML’s Q3 bookings of €2.6B missed expectations.

However, performance from several other key players such as Morgan Stanley (MS) and United Airlines (UAL) have calmed sentiments of market watchers.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII) since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

The S&P 500 Index (SP500) rose 1% in the last one week, while Dow Jones Industrial Average Index (DJI) advanced 1.4%. NASDAQ Composite Index (COMP:IND) rose 0.5% and NASDAQ 100-Index (NDX) fell 0.3%.

data.title: Key takeaways from reports that Uber may be looking to acquire Expedia
data.description:
Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) is being sized up by analysts and investors on reports that the company is looking at an acquisition of Expedia (NASDAQ:EXPE). The talks are believed to be in a very early stage.

Bank of America noted that as a combined company. Uber (UBER) and Expedia (EXPE) could offer bundled travel packages including transportation, accommodation, flights, and even meals while traveling. Analyst Justin Post noted that Uber Travel has already launched flight bookings in countries including the UK, and an Expedia deal could provide a considerable provide even more upside. "Also, while a portion of Uber’s 150mn+ global MAPCs likely already use Expedia, funneling Uber’s user base to Expedia (which has 48mn global unique visitors in the Expedia app, per Expedia Media Solutions) could drive new user/bookings growth for Expedia, especially in International markets," he added. In addition, Uber (UBER) CEO Dara Khosrowshahi’s in-depth experience with Expedia is seen as a positive. In terms of financials, a relatively attractive Expedia free cash flow and EBITDA valuation are also seen as attractive for Uber (UBER).

On the negative side, BofA warned that a potential acquisition of Uber (UBER) could make an already-complex story even more complicated.

Meanwhile, Truist Securities remains skeptical as to the viability of the deal for Uber Technologies (UBER). The firm thinks it would fail to support Uber (UBER) management's stated goal of M&A being first and foremost a driver of growth, since Expedia's (EXPE) projected growth rate is materially below that of Uber's (UBER). The Truist analyst team also thinks potential synergies would be difficult to extract, especially from EXPE's business-to-business segment. The large and complex acquisition is seen adding a layer of risk that could be an operational distraction for Uber (UBER) management at a time when investors are most focused on the risk stemming from the upcoming transition to autonomous vehicles.

Expedia (EXPE) rallied 5.3% in Thursday morning trading, while Uber Technologies (UBER) peeled off 2.7%.

data.title: Guyana accepts production sharing agreements for five offshore oil blocks
data.description:
TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE), QatarEnergy and several other groups have reached agreements with Guyana over terms of production sharing deals to explore five oil and gas offshore blocks, Reuters reported late Wednesday.

A tender launched in 2022 for areas in shallow and deep waters led to offers for eight blocks, part of the government's effort to diversify its energy industry dominated by the Exxon-led (XOM) consortium.

The Exxon (XOM) group, which also is in talks to explore offshore area S8 that is part of the tender, is interested in assessing carbon capture and storage opportunities within the block, the ministry said, according to the report.

A government official said previously that Guyana's cabinet had approved a bid by TotalEnergies (TTE), QatarEnergy and Petronas for exploring shallow water block S4 and negotiations over non-fiscal terms had begun.
